sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/ OR A NUMBER for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Advice on surfing holidays

Jack’s advice
• Recommends surfing for (1) {{BLANK}} holidays in summer

Costs

sections.instruction: Questions 11 and 12: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 13-15: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 16-20: Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 16-20.

questions.content: Which TWO facts are given about the school’s extended hours childcare service?
options: B More children attend after school than before school.
C An average of 50 children attend in the mornings.
D A child cannot attend both the before and after school sessions.
E The maximum number of children who can attend is 70.

questions.content: How much does childcare cost for a complete afternoon session per child?
What does the manager say about food?
options: A Children with allergies should bring their own food.
B Children may bring healthy snacks with them.
C Children are given a proper meal at 5 p.m.

questions.content: What is different about arrangements in the school holidays?
options: A Children from other schools can attend.
C A greater number of children can attend.

questions.content:
What information is given about each of the following activities on offer?
(16) Spanish
(17) Music
(18) Painting
(19) Yoga
(20) Cooking

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 25-30: Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 25-30.

questions.content: Holly has chosen the Orion Stadium placement because
Which aspect of safety does Dr Green emphasise most?
options: A ensuring children stay in the stadium
B checking the equipment children will use
C removing obstacles in changing rooms

questions.content: What does Dr Green say about the spectators?
options: A They can be hard to manage.

questions.content: What has affected the schedule in the past?

What do Holly and her tutor agree is an important aspect of each of the following events management skills?
(25) Communication
(26) Organisation
(27) Time management
(28) Creativity
(29) Leadership
(30) Networking
options: G doing one thing at a time

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
Bird Migration Theory

Most birds are believed to migrate seasonally.

Hibernation theory
• It was believed that birds hibernated underwater or buried themselves in (31) {{BLANK}}
• This theory was later disproved by experiments on caged birds.

Transmutation theory
• Aristotle believed birds changed from one species into another in summer and winter.
– In autumn he observed that redstart’s experience the loss of (32) {{BLANK}} and thought that they turned into robins.
– Aristotle’s assumptions were logical because the two species of birds had a similar (33) {{BLANK}}

17th century
• Charles Morton popularised the idea that birds fly to the (34) {{BLANK}} in winter.

Scientific developments
• In 1822, a stork was killed in Germany which had an African spear in its (35) {{BLANK}}
– previously there had been no (36) {{BLANK}} that storks migrate to Africa
• Little was known about the (37) {{BLANK}} and journeys of migrating birds until the practice of ringing was established.
– It was thought large birds carried small birds on some journeys because they were considered incapable of travelling across huge (38) {{BLANK}}
– Ringing depended on what is called the (39) {{BLANK}} of dead birds.
• In 1931, the first (40) {{BLANK}} to show the migration of European birds was printed.

questions.content:
Bird Migration Theory

Most birds are believed to migrate seasonally.

Hibernation theory
• It was believed that birds hibernated underwater or buried themselves in (31) {{BLANK}}
• This theory was later disproved by experiments on caged birds.

Transmutation theory
• Aristotle believed birds changed from one species into another in summer and winter.
– In autumn he observed that redstart’s experience the loss of (32) {{BLANK}} and thought that they turned into robins.
– Aristotle’s assumptions were logical because the two species of birds had a similar (33) {{BLANK}}

17th century
• Charles Morton popularised the idea that birds fly to the (34) {{BLANK}} in winter.

Scientific developments
• In 1822, a stork was killed in Germany which had an African spear in its (35) {{BLANK}}
– previously there had been no (36) {{BLANK}} that storks migrate to Africa
• Little was known about the (37) {{BLANK}} and journeys of migrating birds until the practice of ringing was established.
– It was thought large birds carried small birds on some journeys because they were considered incapable of travelling across huge (38) {{BLANK}}
– Ringing depended on what is called the (39) {{BLANK}} of dead birds.
• In 1931, the first (40) {{BLANK}} to show the migration of European birds was printed.
